Gameplay Eras Pricing FAQ Play now

Radio strategy across the decades · Beta

Build a Radio Empire

Start in 1970. Launch stations. Hire talent. Chase ratings. Survive format wars, FM disruption, consolidation, and the changing economics of broadcasting.

Beta: create a free account with Clerk to play with unlimited access while we test and tune the game.

Play now — sign in See How It Works
No download • You’ll sign in with Clerk on the next screen
Launch Stations

Pick formats, build brands, and decide whether to chase mass appeal or carve out a niche.

Hire Talent

Build lineups that win listeners — and decide when expensive stars are worth the risk.

Compete for Ratings

Watch rivals react, markets shift, and revenue rise or collapse with your decisions.

Evolve with the Industry

Adapt as AM fades, FM takes over, consolidation changes the business, and new eras rewrite strategy.

Gameplay

Run the Dial. Own the Market.

Every turn forces tradeoffs: format choices, talent costs, branding, programming, debt, and market timing. Build a winner — or watch a once-great station fade.

Compete in Real Markets

Battle through markets like Los Angeles, Atlanta, or Nashville and see how strategy changes by city.

Build Winning Stations

Shape station identity, choose formats, and manage the lineup that drives ratings and revenue.

Live with the Consequences

Great ratings do not guarantee survival. Costs, competition, and timing can wreck a station fast.

The Twist

Radio Changes. You Adapt.

This is not one static board. What works in 1973 may fail by 1993. The business changes underneath you.

1970s

AM still matters

Full-service giants, Top 40 battles, and the old rules of local radio still shape the market.

1980s

FM takes over

Music moves up the dial. AM stations face pressure to reinvent or decline.

1990s

Consolidation hits

Ownership rules change, clusters rise, and strategy becomes more corporate and more ruthless.

2000s+

New pressures

Fragmentation, changing ad markets, and shifting audience habits force hard choices.

Beta access

Play free while we build

During beta, sign in with Clerk for unlimited play at no charge. Paid subscriptions will arrive later for long-term perks (saves, multiplayer, AI assets, and more).

Start Your First Station

Build your lineup. Pick your format. Chase the ratings. See if you can survive long enough to build a broadcasting empire.

Play now

FAQ

Questions

Do I need to know radio to play?

No. If you like strategy, management, and competition, you can play. Radio knowledge helps, but it is not required.

Is this single-player or multiplayer?

Single-player is available during beta. Multiplayer will be part of the future subscription tier when we launch paid plans.

What does beta access include?

Sign in with Clerk for full gameplay and unlimited access while we’re in beta. We’ll add Stripe subscriptions later for saves, multiplayer, AI branding, and other premium features.

Do I need to download anything?

No. Airwave Empire runs right in your browser.
